# HIMSHA Network

> HIMSHA (Hashable Instruction Machine) is an experimental, fully open-source Bitcoin
> programmability layer. Every state transition is proven correct by a RISC Zero
> zero-knowledge (ZK) receipt — not by validator majority vote. It needs no bridges and
> no trust assumptions. This is an educational, proof-of-concept project and is NOT
> production-grade or audited.

## What it is

- A layer for running programs whose correctness is guaranteed by ZK proofs, settled on Bitcoin L1.
- Programs are written in Rust, compiled to RISC-V, and executed inside the RISC Zero zkVM.
- Each execution emits a cryptographic receipt that anyone can verify independently.
- Updated account state is committed to a Bitcoin UTXO, so state survives even if all HIMSHA validators disappear.

## How it works (4 layers)

1. Client builds a signed `RuntimeTransaction` (standard Bitcoin Schnorr keys) targeting one or more programs.
2. The HIMSHA node executes the transaction in the RISC Zero zkVM and produces a verifiable ZK receipt.
3. Updated account state is anchored to a Bitcoin UTXO on L1.
4. If a program produces a Bitcoin transaction (e.g. moving an inscription), the node signs and broadcasts it — no bridge, no wrapped assets.

## Why it differs from other Bitcoin L2s

- Correctness comes from a ZK proof (math), not validator consensus or a fraud-proof window.
- No bridges are required; assets stay on Bitcoin L1.
- ZK makes privacy possible.
- Validator honesty is not required for correctness.

## Built-in programs

- System — account creation, lamport transfer, ownership, allocation.
- Token — fungible tokens (mint, transfer, burn, freeze, delegate).
- ATA — deterministic per-user token accounts.
- Swap — constant-product AMM (x·y=k).
- Lending — borrow BTC against Bitcoin Ordinal inscriptions as collateral (pure time-lock, no oracles).
- NFT Metadata — on-chain name, symbol, URI, royalties, creator verification.
- Runes — Bitcoin Runes fungible tokens (etch, open-mint, transfer, burn).
- Money Market — over-collateralized borrowing with interest accrual and liquidation.

## What you can build

- Ordinals-collateralized lending markets.
- On-chain DEX / AMM for tokens and Runes.
- Token and Runes launchpads.
- NFT marketplaces with enforced on-chain royalties.
- Bitcoin money markets (supply, borrow, repay, liquidate).

## SDKs

- TypeScript (`@himsha-network/sdk`), Python (`himsha-sdk`), and Dart — one shared API surface.

## Key facts

- License/openness: fully open source.
- Proving system: RISC Zero zkVM (RISC-V).
- Settlement layer: Bitcoin L1 (regtest / testnet / mainnet).
- Status: educational proof of concept; not audited; do not use with real mainnet funds.

## Links

- Source code: https://github.com/your-org/himsha-network
- Tutorials: https://your-org.github.io/himsha-network/tutorials.html
- Docker setup: https://your-org.github.io/himsha-network/bitcoin-indexer-docker.html
- Kubernetes & Terraform: https://your-org.github.io/himsha-network/bitcoin-indexer-k8s.html
- RISC Zero: https://risc0.com
